questions.questionText: In a straight line, one angle is 75°. What is the size of the other angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So, the other angle is 180° - 75° = 105°.

questions.questionText: Three angles around a point are 110°, 150°, and x. Find the value of x.
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So, x = 360° - 110° - 150° = 100°.

questions.questionText: The angles in a triangle are 50°, 60°, and y. What is the value of y?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Therefore, y = 180° - 50° - 60° = 70°.

questions.questionText: An isosceles triangle has two equal base angles. If the vertex angle is 50°, what is the size of each base angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. The two base angles are equal. So, each base angle is (180° - 50°) / 2 = 65°.

questions.questionText: A quadrilateral has angles of 90°, 100°, and 75°. What is the size of the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a quadrilateral is 360°. The fourth angle is 360° - 90° - 100° - 75° = 95°.

questions.questionText: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal line. An interior angle is 120°. What is the size of the alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles between parallel lines are equal. So the angle is 120°.

questions.questionText: A transversal intersects two parallel lines, forming a corresponding angle of 85°. What is the measure of the other corresponding angle?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles between parallel lines are equal. The other corresponding angle is also 85°.

questions.questionText: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the co-interior angles is 115°, what is the size of the other co-interior angle?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines add up to 180°. So, the other angle is 180° - 115° = 65°.

questions.questionText: Two straight lines intersect. One of the angles formed is 45°. What is the size of the vertically opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal when two lines intersect. Therefore, the angle is 45°.

questions.questionText: An angle at a point measures 110°. What is the size of the corresponding reflex angle?
questions.explanation: A reflex angle is an angle greater than 180°. It is calculated as 360° minus the given angle. So, 360° - 110° = 250°.

questions.questionText: The exterior angle of a triangle is 130°. One of the interior opposite angles is 60°. Find the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation: The exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles. So, the other angle is 130° - 60° = 70°.

questions.questionText: A straight line intersects a pair of parallel lines. An angle is given as 62°. What is the value of the vertically opposite angle to its corresponding angle?
questions.explanation: The corresponding angle is equal to the given angle (62°). The vertically opposite angle to that corresponding angle is also equal. Thus, the angle is 62°.
